questions.text: Which system of the body includes the heart and blood vessels?
questions.explanation: The heart and blood vessels form the circulatory system.

questions.text: The ____ system helps us breathe and includes the lungs.
questions.explanation: The respiratory system (lungs) helps us breathe.

questions.text: The brain controls all activities of our body.
questions.explanation: The brain is the control centre of the body. True.

questions.text: Which process do plants use to lose extra water through leaves?
questions.explanation: Plants lose extra water through leaves by transpiration.

questions.text: The growing of a seed into a new plant is called ____.
questions.explanation: A seed growing into a plant is germination.

questions.text: Insects help in pollination of flowers.
questions.explanation: Bees and other insects help pollinate flowers. True.

questions.text: Which animal is an amphibian?
questions.explanation: A frog is an amphibian — lives in water and on land.

questions.text: Cold-blooded animals like snakes and lizards are called ____.
questions.explanation: Snakes and lizards are reptiles.

questions.text: Migration means animals moving to another place when seasons change.
questions.explanation: Migration is seasonal movement of animals. True.

questions.text: Which of these is a renewable source of energy?
questions.explanation: Solar energy is renewable (never runs out).

questions.text: Coal and petroleum are ____ fuels that can finish one day.
questions.explanation: Coal and petroleum are fossil fuels (non-renewable).

questions.text: Wind energy is a clean and renewable energy.
questions.explanation: Wind energy is clean and renewable. True.

questions.text: The continuous movement of water between Earth and sky is called the ____.
questions.explanation: It is called the water cycle.

questions.text: Collecting and storing rainwater is called rainwater ____.
questions.explanation: Storing rainwater is rainwater harvesting.

questions.text: Most of the Earth's surface is covered with water.
questions.explanation: About 71% of Earth is covered with water. True.

questions.text: Which gas is mainly responsible for air pollution from vehicles?
questions.explanation: Vehicles release harmful carbon monoxide.

questions.text: Planting trees on a large scale is called ____.
questions.explanation: Planting many trees is afforestation.

questions.text: Loud noise from horns and machines causes noise pollution.
questions.explanation: Excess noise is noise pollution. True.

questions.text: Which is the longest river that flows through India?
questions.explanation: The Ganga is the longest river in India.

questions.text: A ____ is a drawing of the Earth's surface showing places and directions.
questions.explanation: A map shows places and directions on a flat surface.

questions.text: A globe is a model of the Earth.
questions.explanation: A globe is a round model of the Earth. True.

questions.text: Lack of which vitamin causes night blindness?
questions.explanation: Vitamin A deficiency causes night blindness.

questions.text: Lack of iron in food can cause a disease called ____.
questions.explanation: Iron deficiency causes anaemia.

questions.text: Sunlight helps our body make vitamin D.
questions.explanation: Sunlight helps make vitamin D. True.

questions.text: Which instrument is used to measure rainfall?
questions.explanation: A rain gauge measures rainfall.

questions.text: A ____ measures air pressure.
questions.explanation: A barometer measures air pressure.

questions.text: Soil is an important natural resource for farming.
questions.explanation: Soil is vital for growing crops. True.

questions.text: Which part of the eye controls how much light enters?
questions.explanation: The pupil changes size to control light entering the eye.

questions.text: Some plants like the venus flytrap eat insects.
questions.explanation: Insect-eating plants exist (e.g., venus flytrap). True.

questions.text: Which of these helps reduce waste?
questions.explanation: Recycling reduces waste and saves resources.
